title: Missing root-path jail check after path normalization and symlink resolution
description: The real(path:) method (line 26-28) simply concatenates rootPath with the user-supplied path: 'self.rootPath.stringByAppendingPathComponent(path: path)'. Even where symlinks are resolved (getInfo, setPermissions, move), the code never verifies that the resolved absolute path is still within rootPath. An attacker who manages to create a symlink inside the root pointing outside (or exploits a TOCTOU race between normalization and the filesystem operation) can escape the file jail entirely. There is no single enforcement point that guarantees all resolved paths remain under rootPath.
recommendation: Add a jailCheck method: after resolving symlinks, verify that resolvedPath.hasPrefix(self.rootPath + '/') || resolvedPath == self.rootPath. Call this check in every handler before performing filesystem operations. Return an error (permission_denied or file_not_found) if the check fails.
